message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Share global Claude Code skills via GitHub repository (`https://github.com/designer976/Skills-e-Agents.git`)
   - Review and update each skill using external repos from `npx skills add` commands and `skills.sh`
   - Maintain automatic git commit/push flow after each update (user explicitly approved this)
   - All skills should remain project-specific (Easy Salon — NestJS + React + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Claude Code Skills system (`~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md`)
   - Git initialization and remote push to GitHub
   - NestJS + TypeScript backend patterns (N+1, retry, throttler, structured logging)
   - React + Vite + Tailwind + shadcn/ui frontend patterns
   - React Hook Form + Zod form validation
   - TanStack Query (React Query) data fetching
   - MSW (Mock Service Worker) for API mocking in tests
   - Prisma ORM soft delete pattern
   - OWASP Top 10 security checks for NestJS
   - Vercel composition patterns (boolean props, compound components)
   - React performance patterns (derived state, lazy init, Error Boundary, React.lazy)
   - Design system token enforcement (shadcn/ui, Tailwind tokens)
   - Responsive design (breakpoints, touch targets 44px, dark mode)
   - Component selection guide (AlertDialog vs Dialog vs Sheet vs Toast)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`C:/Users/charl/.claude/skills/README.md`** (created)
     - Installation instructions, skill list table, auto-activation rules for CLAUDE.md
     - Install command: `git clone https://github.com/designer976/Skills-e-Agents.git ~/.claude/skills`

   - **`C:/Users/charl/.claude/skills/backend/SKILL.md`** (updated)
     - Added "Referências de Padrões" section with:
       - N+1 Query Prevention (batch fetch with Map)
       - Retry com Exponential Backoff (`withRetry` function)
       - Rate Limiting via `@nestjs/throttler`
       - Structured Logging with NestJS Logger

   - **`C:/Users/charl/.claude/skills/project-rules/SKILL.md`** (updated)
     - Added Section 9: React Hook Form + Zod pattern (schema, `useForm`, error display, submit button with `isSubmitting`)
     - Added Section 10: TanStack Query patterns (`queryKey` rules, `invalidateQueries` after mutations, `isPending` for buttons)

   - **`C:/Users/charl/.claude/skills/tester/SKILL.md`** (updated)
     - Added MSW (Mock Service Worker) section under "Testes de Componentes React"
     - `handlers.ts`, `server.ts`, `vitest.setup.ts` setup pattern
     - Rule: use MSW for network-level mocking, `jest.mock` only for non-network modules

   - **`C:/Users/charl/.claude/skills/front-end-ui/SKILL.md`** (updated)
     - Added Passo 4b: Estados Obrigatórios (loading skeleton, empty state with action, error state with message)
     - Added Passo 6: Lazy Loading — `React.lazy` + `Suspense` for components >50kb, on-demand modals; with when/when-not rules

   - **`C:/Users/charl/.claude/skills/database/SKILL.md`** (updated)
     - Added Soft Delete pattern under "Consultas Eficientes"
     - Prisma example with `deletedAt DateTime?`, update to set date, findMany with `deletedAt: null` filter
     - Warning: update existing queries when adding `deletedAt` to existing entities

   - **`C:/Users/charl/.claude/skills/reviewer/SKILL.md`** (updated)
     - Replaced generic security section with OWASP Top 10 mapped to project context
     - A01 (Broken Access Control), A02 (Cryptographic Failures), A03 (Injection), A05 (Security Misconfiguration), A07 (Auth Failures), A09 (Logging Failures)
     - NestJS-specific checks: helmet, throttler on auth endpoints, `ValidationPipe` with `whitelist: true`, no unprotected routes, `.env` validation

   - **`C:/Users/charl/.claude/skills/front-end-code/SKILL.md`** (updated — multiple passes)
     - Added Section 13: Arquitetura de Componentes:
       - No inline components (causes re-mount every render) with example
       - No boolean prop proliferation (>2 booleans → use `cva` or compound components)
       - Conditional render: ternary not `&&` (avoids rendering `0` as text)
       - Direct imports over barrel files
     - Added to Anti-patterns: inline component definition, `{value && <Comp />}` when value can be `0`
     - Added to Section 1 (Performance):
       - `useEffect` for derived state anti-pattern with ❌/✅ examples
       - Lazy state initialization: `useState(() => fn())` vs `useState(fn())`
     - Added Section 14: Error Boundary — when 🟡 Alerta vs 🔴 Crítico, Shadcn components without built-in boundary

   - **`C:/Users/charl/.claude/skills/designer/SKILL.md`** (updated)
     - Fase 1: Added touch target minimum 44×44px and no-hover-on-mobile rule
     - Added Passo 2b: Tailwind breakpoint reference table (base/sm/md/lg/xl) with mobile-first rule and container queries note
     - Added Passo 2c: Dark mode awareness (`darkMode: 'class'` check, `dark:` variants requirement)
     - Fase 3: Added component selection guide table (AlertDialog/Dialog/Sheet/Toast/Alert/DropdownMenu/Select/Combobox)

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - Several external repos returned 404 (supercent-io UI design skills, wshobson tailwind-design-system, clerk/skills) — resolved by searching tree structure first before fetching content
   - `git init` in skills folder initially set cwd back to project folder — resolved by using full absolute path in subsequent commands
   - LF/CRLF warnings on Windows — non-blocking, ignored (git handles this automatically)

5. Problem Solving:
   - Identified which external skills were actually accessible vs 404 before attempting content fetch
   - Filtered Next.js-specific rules (RSC, server actions, useSearchParams) from Vercel's 65-rule list to keep only Vite-compatible rules
   - Kept all skills project-specific (Portuguese language, NestJS/shadcn/ui context) rather than replacing with generic English skills
   - Evaluated `frontend-design` (Anthropic) as incompatible — it promotes DS-free creative design, conflicts with strict DS enforcement workflow

6. All User Messages:
   - "quero compartilhar minhas skills globais, como eu faço?"
   - "criei um repositório 'https://github.com/designer976/Skills-e-Agents.git'"
   - "verifique se precisamos atualizar o Backend: [6 npx skills add commands]"
   - "sim" (approve incorporating N+1, retry, rate limiting, logging patterns)
   - "das skills e agentes que temos, alguma atualização que deseja indicar?"
   - "pode" (approve all 5 skill improvements)
   - "Com base nesse site, algo que possa ser interessante para usar na atualização? https://skills.sh/"
   - "pode continuar" (approve vercel-composition-patterns + react-best-practices additions to front-end-code)
   - "no designer, algo a atualizar que seja interessante?"
   - "pode" (approve 4 designer improvements)
   - "no front-end temos algo para atualizar?"
   - "sim, se acha importante" (approve 4 frontend improvements)
   - "Atualize o git hub https://github.com/designer976/Skills-e-Agents.git"
   - "incluindo o que atualizamos aqui recentemente?"
   - "o commit foi feito automatico:?"
   - "pode manter assim" (approve automatic commit/push flow)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - None explicitly pending. All requested updates have been applied and pushed.

8. Current Work:
   The last substantive work was confirming the GitHub repository `https://github.com/designer976/Skills-e-Agents.git` is fully up to date with all 6 commits from this session:
   - `02cb1d4` — Initial push of all 10 skills + README
   - `e1f5b5d` — backend skill enrichment
   - `967d12e` — project-rules, tester, front-end-ui, database, reviewer improvements
   - `d4685c5` — front-end-code component architecture
   - `b862b5b` — designer improvements
   - `38f5682` — front-end-code + front-end-ui performance/reliability patterns

   User asked "o commit foi feito automatico:?" → confirmed yes, automatic. User approved: "pode manter assim".
   Then user asked to confirm the GitHub is up to date including recent updates — confirmed with `git status` showing `nothing to commit, working tree clean`.

9. Optional Next Step:
   No pending tasks. All updates have been applied and pushed. The user's last message was confirming the automatic commit flow is acceptable ("pode manter assim"). No further action is required unless the user requests additional skill reviews or updates.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\charl\.claude\projects\c--Users-charl-OneDrive--rea-de-Trabalho-WA-Easy\449790ba-67f6-4c08-9f41-82bcd423e30b.jsonl
